mission.title: traefik: Add a provider for Key Value (KV) Store in Traefik v2
mission.description: Add a provider for Key Value (KV) Store in Traefik v2. This issue affects 39+ users.

steps.title: Identify traefik feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your traefik deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=traefik
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Add a provider for Key Value (KV) Store in Traefik v2

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant traefik resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
### Do you want to request a *feature* or report a *bug*?

Feature

### What did you expect to see?

Traefik v1 supports dynamic config generation for and from a Key Value Store, i.e. it has a kv prov

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add a provider for Key Value (KV) Store in Traefik v2
steps.description:
### What does this PR do?

Remove reference to KV storage for Traefik CE. Per https://github.com/containous/traefik/issues/5426

### More

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Add a provider for Key Value (KV) Store in Traefik is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: ### What does this PR do?

Remove reference to KV storage for Traefik CE. Per https://github.com/containous/traefik/issues/5426

### More. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with traefik installed or the issue environment reproducible.
